title: Build Admin Dashboard with full platform control for Prodigy Digital Library

requirements.text: Extend the backend Motoko actor to support admin-only operations gated to principal ID `zhpm4-xby3w-hy7nv-ziwxz-heeqw-f2qf4-p2o5a-qradb`: get all users with activity info, delete a user, ban/unban a user, suspend/unsuspend a user; delete any book regardless of owner; approve or reject a book (set approval status); feature/unfeature a book on the homepage; get all uploaded PDFs with metadata; delete or replace a PDF; get storage usage stats and enforce a configurable max upload size; get and set platform settings (site name, logo URL, announcement banner text, maintenance mode toggle); force-logout a user (invalidate session marker); get analytics data (total users, total books, total uploads, most-viewed books, most-bookmarked books, new users and books over time). All admin endpoints must verify the caller's principal matches the hardcoded admin principal and trap/return an error otherwise.

quotes: Only Admin can: Access Admin Dashboard, Change core settings, Delete the platform content, Control storage system, Manage backend functionality
Admin Principal ID is the controller identity

acceptanceCriteria: Calling any admin endpoint with a non-admin principal returns an access-denied error and does not mutate state.
Calling admin endpoints with principal `zhpm4-xby3w-hy7nv-ziwxz-heeqw-f2qf4-p2o5a-qradb` succeeds and mutates state as expected.
banUser and suspendUser mark the user record accordingly; bannedusers cannot perform authenticated actions.
getPlatformSettings and setPlatformSettings correctly read and write site name, logo URL, announcement text, and maintenance mode flag.
getAnalytics returns total user count, total book count, most-viewed books list, and most-bookmarked books list.
getStorageStats returns aggregate PDF storage bytes used and the current configured max upload size.
featureBook marks a book as featured; featured books can be queried separately for homepage display.

requirements.text: Build a complete Admin Dashboard page at the `/admin` route, protected by the existing `AdminGuard` component. The dashboard must contain clearly separated sections reachable via a sidebar or tab navigation: Overview/Analytics, User Management, Book Management, Storage Control, Platform Settings, and Security. The UI must be clean, modern, and fully mobile-responsive.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to `/admin` while not logged in or not the admin principal shows the access-denied UI from AdminGuard.
Navigating to `/admin` as the admin principal renders the Admin Dashboard with all sections accessible.
The dashboard is usable on mobile viewports (320px and up) with a collapsible sidebar or bottom tab navigation.
Each section loads its data independently with loading skeletons and error states.

requirements.text: Implement the Analytics/Overview section of the Admin Dashboard. Display stat cards for: total registered users, total books, total uploads, total approved books, total pending books. Display a list of the most-viewed books and most-bookmarked books. Display most-active users by upload count.

quotes: Show total users, books, and uploads

acceptanceCriteria: Stat cards show correct counts fetched from the backend getAnalytics/adminStats endpoints.
Most-viewed and most-bookmarked book lists render book title and count.
Most-active users list renders display name and upload count.
All data refreshes when the admin revisits the section.

requirements.text: Implement the User Management section of the Admin Dashboard. Show a paginated or scrollable table of all registered users with columns: display name, principal ID, role, status (active/banned/suspended), books uploaded, join date. Provide action buttons per row to: delete user, ban/unban user, suspend/unsuspend user. Confirm destructive actions with a confirmation dialog before calling the backend.

quotes: See user activity (books uploaded, reading activity)

acceptanceCriteria: User table populates with all registered users from the backend.
Ban/unban button label toggles based on current user status.
Delete action shows a confirmation dialog; confirming calls deleteUser and removes the row.
Toast notifications confirm success or display errors for each action.

requirements.text: Implement the Book Management section of the Admin Dashboard. Show a searchable, filterable list of all books (including pending and approved) with cover thumbnail, title, author, genre, status (approved/pending/rejected), featured flag, and view count. Provide per-book actions: edit (title, description, genre, cover URL), delete, approve, reject, feature/unfeature. The admin upload form should reuse or mirror the existing Upload page form.

quotes: Edit any book (title, description, genre, cover, PDF)

acceptanceCriteria: All books regardless of status appear in the admin book list.
Approve/reject buttons update the book's approval status and re-fetch the list.
Feature/unfeature toggle updates the book's featured flag.
Edit form is pre-populated with the book's current data and saves changes to the backend.
Delete action requires confirmation before removing the book.

requirements.text: Implement the Storage Control section of the Admin Dashboard. Display aggregate storage usage (total bytes used across all PDFs) and the configured maximum upload size. Show a list of all uploaded PDF entries with file name, associated book title, uploader, file size, and upload date. Provide a delete button per PDF entry and a global control to update the maximum allowed upload size.
acceptanceCriteria: Storage stats card shows total bytes used and current max upload size.
PDF list shows all entries with metadata from the backend.
Deleting a PDF entry calls the backend, removes it from the list, and updates the storage stats.
Admin can update the max upload size via an inline input; new value is persisted to the backend.

requirements.text: Implement the Platform Settings section of the Admin Dashboard. Provide an editable form with fields for: site name, logo URL, homepage announcement banner text (with an enable/disable toggle), and a maintenance mode toggle switch. Saving the form calls the backend setPlatformSettings endpoint. When maintenance mode is enabled, non-admin users visiting the frontend should see a maintenance page instead of the normal app.

quotes: Control homepage content and featured sections

acceptanceCriteria: Platform settings form loads current values from the backend on mount.
Saving the form persists all fields to the backend and shows a success toast.
When maintenance mode is on, visiting any non-admin route shows a maintenance-mode interstitial page with the platform name and an optional message.
Admin visiting `/admin` bypasses the maintenance-mode interstitial.
Announcement banner text appears on the homepage when enabled.

requirements.text: Implement the Security section of the Admin Dashboard. Display a list of currently banned and suspended users with their status and the ability to unban/unsuspend from this view. Add a searchable account-blocking form where the admin can enter a principal ID to block (ban) that account. Display a platform activity log showing recent events: new user registrations, book uploads, and books removed — with timestamps and actor principal.
acceptanceCriteria: Banned/suspended users list is populated from the backend and allows quick status reversal.
Block-by-principal form validates the input and calls banUser for the entered principal.
Activity log shows at least the 20 most recent events with event type, principal, and timestamp.
All security actions show success/failure toast notifications.

constraints: Admin principal ID `zhpm4-xby3w-hy7nv-ziwxz-heeqw-f2qf4-p2o5a-qradb` must be hardcoded in the backend as the sole authorized admin caller.
All admin backend endpoints must trap or return an error variant if called by any principal other than the admin principal.
Do not modify files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Keep all backend logic in the single main actor at backend/main.mo.
Reuse the existing AdminGuard component for route protection — do not replace it.
nonGoals: Multi-admin or role-based admin tiers — only one hardcoded admin principal.
Real-time activity monitoring via WebSockets or polling — static log fetch is sufficient.
Email or SMS notifications for admin actions.
Canister upgrade or IC management API calls from within the app.
